
```{r}
loan_data <- read.csv("loan_approval_dataset.csv")
str(loan_data)
summary(loan_data)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(caret)

set.seed(123) 

n <- 1000
loan_data <- data.frame(
  CIBIL_score = round(rnorm(n, mean = 700, sd = 50)),
  Income = round(exp(rnorm(n, mean = 10, sd = 1))),
  Employment = sample(c("Salaried", "Self-employed", "Unemployed"), n, replace = TRUE),
  Loan_term = sample(c(12, 24, 36, 48, 60), n, replace = TRUE),
  Loan_amount = round(exp(rnorm(n, mean = 8, sd = 0.8))),
  Assets_value = round(exp(rnorm(n, mean = 9, sd = 1.2)))
)
loan_data <- loan_data %>%
  mutate(probability_approved = plogis((CIBIL_score - 650)/50 + log(Income) - 10 - (Loan_amount - 2000)/1000)) %>%
  mutate(Loan_status = factor(ifelse(runif(n) < probability_approved, "Approved", "Rejected"))) %>%
  select(-probability_approved)
print("Distribution in full loan_data:")
print(table(loan_data$Loan_status))
```

```{r}
# Data Preprocessing
loan_data <- loan_data %>%
  mutate(Employment = as.factor(Employment))

# Check for missing values
missing_values <- colSums(is.na(loan_data))
print(missing_values)

# Handle outliers using the IQR method
handle_outliers_iqr <- function(x) {
  Q1 <- quantile(x, 0.25)
  Q3 <- quantile(x, 0.75)
  IQR <- Q3 - Q1
  lower_bound <- Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
  upper_bound <- Q3 + 1.5 * IQR
  x[x < lower_bound] <- lower_bound
  x[x > upper_bound] <- upper_bound
  return(x)
}
# Apply outlier handling to numerical columns
numerical_cols <- names(select_if(loan_data, is.numeric))
loan_data[numerical_cols] <- lapply(loan_data[numerical_cols], handle_outliers_iqr)

```

```{r}
# Split data
set.seed(456)
trainIndex <- createDataPartition(loan_data$Loan_status, p = 0.7, list = FALSE)
train_data <- loan_data[trainIndex, ]
test_data <- loan_data[-trainIndex, ]
print("\nDistribution in training data:")
print(table(train_data$Loan_status))
print("\nDistribution in test data:")
print(table(test_data$Loan_status))
```

```{r}
# Exploratory Data Analysis (EDA)

# Histograms of numerical features
par(mfrow = c(2, 3)) 
numerical_cols <- names(select_if(loan_data, is.numeric))
for (col in numerical_cols) {
  hist(loan_data[[col]], main = paste("Histogram of", col), xlab = col, col = "lightblue", border = "white")
}
par(mfrow = c(1, 1)) 
print("Displaying histograms")

# Bar plot of Employment 
counts <- table(loan_data$Employment, loan_data$Loan_status)
barplot(counts, main = "Loan Status by Employment Type",
        xlab = "Loan Status", ylab = "Frequency",
        legend = rownames(counts), beside = TRUE)
print("Displaying Employment vs Loan Status")

# Box plots of numerical features by Loan_status
par(mfrow = c(2, 3)) 
numerical_cols <- names(select_if(loan_data, is.numeric))
for (col in numerical_cols) {
  boxplot(loan_data[[col]] ~ loan_data$Loan_status, main = paste("Boxplot of", col),
          ylab = col, xlab = "Loan Status")
}
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
print("Displaying numerical features vs Loan Status")
```

```{r}
# 3. Exploratory Data Analysis and Visualization
# a. Numeric vs. Numeric: Pearson Correlation and Scatter Plots
numeric_vars <- c("CIBIL_score", "Income", "Loan_term", "Loan_amount", "Assets_value")
cor_matrix <- cor(train_data[, numeric_vars], method = "pearson")
print("Pearson Correlation Matrix (Numeric vs. Numeric):")
print(cor_matrix)
for (i in 1:(length(numeric_vars) - 1)) {
  for (j in (i + 1):length(numeric_vars)) {
    x_var <- numeric_vars[i]
    y_var <- numeric_vars[j]
    plot(train_data[[x_var]], train_data[[y_var]],
         xlab = x_var, ylab = y_var,
         main = paste("Scatter Plot of", x_var, "vs.", y_var))
    abline(lm(train_data[[y_var]] ~ train_data[[x_var]]))
   
  }
}
```

```{r}
# b. Ordinal vs. Ordinal: Kendall/Spearman Correlation and Scatter Plots (Loan_term is treated as ordinal)
kendall_corr <- cor(train_data$Loan_term, as.numeric(train_data$Loan_term), method = "kendall")
spearman_corr <- cor(train_data$Loan_term, as.numeric(train_data$Loan_term), method = "spearman")
cat("\nKendall Correlation for Loan_term:", kendall_corr, "\n")
cat("Spearman Correlation for Loan_term:", spearman_corr, "\n")

plot(train_data$Loan_term, as.numeric(train_data$Loan_term),
     xlab = "Loan_term", ylab = "Numeric Loan_term",
     main = "Scatter Plot of Loan_term vs. Numeric Loan_term")
abline(lm(as.numeric(train_data$Loan_term) ~ train_data$Loan_term))
```

```{r}
# c. Nominal vs. Nominal: Stacked Bar Chart and Chi-Square Test
contingency_table <- table(train_data$Employment, train_data$Loan_status)
proportions <- prop.table(contingency_table, 1) 
barplot(t(proportions), 
        col = c("white", "lightblue", "gray"), 
        legend.text = rownames(proportions),
        xlab = "Employment",
        ylab = "Proportion",
        main = "Stacked Bar Chart of Employment vs. Loan Status"
        )

chi2_test <- chisq.test(contingency_table)
print("Chi-Square Test Results for Employment and Loan Status:")
print(chi2_test)
```

```{r}
# d. Nominal vs. Numeric: Box Plots
for (i in 1:length(numeric_vars)) {
  x_var <- "Employment"
  y_var <- numeric_vars[i]
  boxplot(train_data[[y_var]] ~ train_data[[x_var]],
          xlab = x_var, ylab = y_var,
          main = paste("Box Plot of", y_var, "by", x_var),
          col = c("lightblue", "lightgreen", "lightyellow")
          )

}
```

```{r}
# Shuffle the data randomly
set.seed(789) 
loan_data_shuffled <- loan_data[sample(nrow(loan_data)), ]
(loan_data_shuffled)
```

```{r}
# Modle 1: knn model
trainIndex_knn <- createDataPartition(loan_data_shuffled$Loan_status, p = 0.8, list = FALSE)
train_data_knn <- loan_data_shuffled[trainIndex_knn, ]
test_data_knn <- loan_data_shuffled[-trainIndex_knn, ]

# Separate features and labels
X_train_knn <- train_data_knn %>% select(-Loan_status)
y_train_knn <- train_data_knn$Loan_status
X_test_knn <- test_data_knn %>% select(-Loan_status)
y_test_knn <- test_data_knn$Loan_status

# min-max normalization 
normalize=function(train,val){
  train_col_mins= sapply(train, min)
  train_col_maxs= sapply(train,max)
  min_max_normalize=function(x, min, max){
    return ((x-min)/(max-min))}
  train_n=as.data.frame(mapply(min_max_normalize, train, train_col_mins, train_col_maxs ))
  val_n=as.data.frame(mapply(min_max_normalize, val, train_col_mins, train_col_maxs ))
  return (list("train_n"=train_n, "val_n"=val_n))
}

knn_fold=function(features,labels,fold,kneighbors){
  train=features[-fold,]
  val=features[fold,]
  data_n= normalize(train, val)
  train_n= data_n$train_n
  val_n= data_n$val_n
  train_labels=labels[-fold]
  validation_labels=labels[fold]
  val_preds=knn(train_n,val_n,train_labels,k=kneighbors)
  t= table(validation_labels,val_preds)
  error=(t[1,2]+t[2,1])/sum(t)
  return(error)
}
```

```{r}
library(recipes)
print("K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN)")
recipe_knn <- recipe(Loan_status ~ ., data = train_data) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

prepped_recipe_knn <- prep(recipe_knn, training = train_data)
train_processed_knn <- bake(prepped_recipe_knn, new_data = train_data)
test_processed_knn <- bake(prepped_recipe_knn, new_data = test_data)

# Train KNN
model_knn <- train(
  Loan_status ~ .,
  data = train_processed_knn,
  method = "knn",
  trControl = trainControl(method = "cv", number = 5),
  tuneGrid = data.frame(k = c(3, 5, 7, 9)),
  metric = "Accuracy"
)
print(model_knn)
```
```{r}
#install.packages("ROCR")
library(ROCR)
predictions_knn <- predict(model_knn, newdata = test_processed_knn)
cm_knn <- confusionMatrix(predictions_knn, test_processed_knn$Loan_status)
print("KNN Confusion Matrix:")
print(cm_knn)

accuracy_knn <- cm_knn$overall["Accuracy"]
error_rate_knn <- 1 - accuracy_knn
cat(paste("\nKNN Accuracy:", round(accuracy_knn, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("KNN Error Rate:", round(error_rate_knn, 4), "\n"))

precision_knn <- cm_knn$byClass["Precision"]
recall_knn <- cm_knn$byClass["Recall"]
f1_knn <- cm_knn$byClass["F1"]
cat(paste("KNN Precision:", round(precision_knn, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("KNN Recall:", round(recall_knn, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("KNN F1-score:", round(f1_knn, 4), "\n"))

plot(as.table(cm_knn$table), main = "Confusion Matrix (KNN)",
     xlab = "Predicted", ylab = "Actual")
print("Displaying KNN Confusion Matrix visually")
```

```{r}
library(pROC)
roc_knn <- roc(test_processed_knn$Loan_status, as.numeric(predictions_knn))
plot(roc_knn, main = "ROC Curve for KNN")
cat("AUC for KNN:", auc(roc_knn), "\n")
```
```{r}
# Model 2: Logistic Regression
cat("Logistic Regression")
model_lr <- glm(Loan_status ~ ., data = train_data, family = binomial)
summary(model_lr) 
predictions_lr_prob <- predict(model_lr, newdata = test_data, type = "response")
predicted_classes_lr <- factor(ifelse(predictions_lr_prob > 0.5, "Approved", "Rejected"), levels = levels(train_data$Loan_status))

# Evaluate the model
conf_matrix_lr <- confusionMatrix(predicted_classes_lr, test_data$Loan_status)
print(conf_matrix_lr) # Print the confusion matrix
accuracy_lr <- conf_matrix_lr$overall["Accuracy"]
error_rate_lr <- 1 - accuracy_lr
cat(paste("\nLogistic Regression Accuracy:", round(accuracy_lr, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("Logistic Regression Error Rate:", round(error_rate_lr, 4), "\n"))

precision_lr <- conf_matrix_lr$byClass["Precision"]
recall_lr <- conf_matrix_lr$byClass["Recall"]
f1_lr <- conf_matrix_lr$byClass["F1"]
cat(paste("Precision:", round(precision_lr, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("Recall:", round(recall_lr, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("F1-score:", round(f1_lr, 4), "\n"))
```

```{r}
cat("Bar Plot of Predicted vs. Actual Classes")
plot_data_lr <- data.frame(
  Actual = test_data$Loan_status,
  Predicted = predicted_classes_lr
)
class_counts_lr <- table(plot_data_lr$Actual, plot_data_lr$Predicted)

barplot_matrix_lr <- matrix(
  c(class_counts_lr[1, ], class_counts_lr[2, ]), 
  nrow = 2,
  byrow = TRUE,
  dimnames = list(c("Actual Approved", "Actual Rejected"), c("Predicted Approved", "Predicted Rejected"))
)
barplot(
  barplot_matrix_lr,
  beside = TRUE,
  legend.text = rownames(barplot_matrix_lr),
  main = "Predicted vs. Actual Loan Status (Logistic Regression)",
  ylab = "Frequency",
  xlab = "Predicted Loan Status"
)
```

```{r}
roc_lr <- roc(test_data$Loan_status, predictions_lr_prob)
plot(roc_lr, main = "ROC Curve for Logistic Regression")
cat("AUC for Logistic Regression:", auc(roc_lr), "\n")
```


```{r}
# Model 3: Support Vector Machines (SVM) 
cat("Support Vector Machines (SVM)")
library(e1071)
recipe_svm <- recipe(Loan_status ~ ., data = train_data) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors()) %>%
  step_normalize(all_numeric_predictors())

prepped_recipe_svm <- prep(recipe_svm, training = train_data)
train_processed_svm <- bake(prepped_recipe_svm, new_data = train_data)
test_processed_svm <- bake(prepped_recipe_svm, new_data = test_data)

model_svm <- svm(Loan_status ~ ., data = train_processed_svm, kernel = "linear", probability = TRUE) 
print(model_svm)

predictions_svm <- predict(model_svm, newdata = test_processed_svm, probability = TRUE)
predictions_svm_prob_values <- attr(predictions_svm, "probabilities")

# Evaluate SVM
cm_svm <- confusionMatrix(predictions_svm, test_processed_svm$Loan_status)
print("SVM Confusion Matrix:")
print(cm_svm)

accuracy_svm <- cm_svm$overall["Accuracy"]
error_rate_svm <- 1 - accuracy_svm
cat(paste("\nSVM Accuracy:", round(accuracy_svm, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("SVM Error Rate:", round(error_rate_svm, 4), "\n"))

precision_svm <- cm_svm$byClass["Precision"]
recall_svm <- cm_svm$byClass["Recall"]
f1_svm <- cm_svm$byClass["F1"]
cat(paste("SVM Precision:", round(precision_svm, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("SVM Recall:", round(recall_svm, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("SVM F1-score:", round(f1_svm, 4), "\n"))

plot(as.table(cm_svm$table), main = "Confusion Matrix (SVM)",
     xlab = "Predicted", ylab = "Actual")
print("Displaying SVM Confusion Matrix visually")
```

```{r}
roc_svm <- roc(test_processed_svm$Loan_status, predictions_svm_prob_values[,2]) 
plot(roc_svm, main = "ROC Curve for SVM")
cat("AUC for SVM:", auc(roc_svm), "\n")
```
```{r}
#install.packages("xgboost")
library(xgboost)
cat("Gradient Boosting (XGBoost)")

recipe_xgb <- recipe(Loan_status ~ ., data = train_data) %>%
  step_dummy(all_nominal_predictors())
prepped_recipe_xgb <- prep(recipe_xgb, training = train_data)
train_processed_xgb <- bake(prepped_recipe_xgb, new_data = train_data)
test_processed_xgb <- bake(prepped_recipe_xgb, new_data = test_data)

X_train_xgb <- train_processed_xgb %>% select(-Loan_status)
y_train_xgb <- as.numeric(train_processed_xgb$Loan_status) - 1
X_test_xgb <- test_processed_xgb %>% select(-Loan_status)
y_test_xgb <- as.numeric(test_processed_xgb$Loan_status) - 1

# Train XGBoost model
model_xgb <- xgboost(
  data = as.matrix(X_train_xgb),
  label = y_train_xgb,
  nrounds = 100, 
  objective = "binary:logistic",
  eval_metric = "logloss"
)

predictions_xgb_prob <- predict(model_xgb, newdata = as.matrix(X_test_xgb))
predicted_classes_xgb <- factor(ifelse(predictions_xgb_prob > 0.5, "Approved", "Rejected"), levels = levels(train_data$Loan_status))

cm_xgb <- confusionMatrix(predicted_classes_xgb, test_data$Loan_status)
print("XGBoost Confusion Matrix:")
print(cm_xgb)

accuracy_xgb <- cm_xgb$overall["Accuracy"]
error_rate_xgb <- 1 - accuracy_xgb
cat(paste("\nXGBoost Accuracy:", round(accuracy_xgb, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("XGBoost Error Rate:", round(error_rate_xgb, 4), "\n"))

precision_xgb <- cm_xgb$byClass["Precision"]
recall_xgb <- cm_xgb$byClass["Recall"]
f1_xgb <- cm_xgb$byClass["F1"]
cat(paste("XGB Precision:", round(precision_xgb, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("XGB Recall:", round(recall_xgb, 4), "\n"))
cat(paste("XGB F1-score:", round(f1_xgb, 4), "\n"))

plot(as.table(cm_xgb$table), main = "Confusion Matrix (XGBoost)",
     xlab = "Predicted", ylab = "Actual")
print("Displaying XGBoost Confusion Matrix visually")
```
```{r}
roc_xgb <- roc(test_data$Loan_status, predictions_xgb_prob) 
plot(roc_xgb, main = "ROC Curve for XGBoost")
cat("AUC for XGBoost:", auc(roc_xgb), "\n")
```

```{r}
# Model Comparison
cat("Model Comparison")
library(dplyr)
model_metrics <- data.frame(
  Model = c("KNN", "Logistic Regression", "SVM", "XGBoost"),
  Accuracy = c(accuracy_knn, accuracy_lr, accuracy_svm, accuracy_xgb),
  Error_Rate = c(error_rate_knn, error_rate_lr, error_rate_svm, error_rate_xgb),
  Precision = c(precision_knn, precision_lr, precision_svm, precision_xgb),
  Recall = c(recall_knn, recall_lr, recall_svm, recall_xgb),
  F1_Score = c(f1_knn, f1_lr, f1_svm, f1_xgb)
)
print(model_metrics)
ranked_models <- model_metrics %>%
  arrange(desc(F1_Score))
print(ranked_models)
```

```{r}
model_metrics <- model_metrics[order(model_metrics$F1_Score, decreasing = TRUE), ]
colors <- c("KNN" = "lightblue", "Logistic Regression" = "lightyellow", "SVM" = "lightpink", "XGBoost" = "gray")
bar_colors <- colors[model_metrics$Model] # Get colors in the correct order

barplot(
  height = model_metrics$F1_Score,
  names.arg = model_metrics$Model,
  col = bar_colors,
  ylim = c(0, 1),
  main = "Model Comparison - F1 Score",
  xlab = "Model",
  ylab = "F1 Score"
)
text(
  x = 1:nrow(model_metrics),
  y = model_metrics$F1_Score + 0.05, 
  labels = round(model_metrics$F1_Score, 3),
  cex = 0.8
)
legend(
  "topright",
  legend = model_metrics$Model,
  fill = bar_colors,
  bty = "n" 
)
```
